title: Arum alpinariae
taxonomy: Arum alpinariae is classified under the family Araceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the arum family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Aroideae subfamily, to which Arum alpinariae belongs. Within the Aroideae subfamily, Arum alpinariae is placed in the tribe Arumae. The specific classification of Arum alpinariae is Arum alpinariae (Cirillo) P. M. Opiz, which is a synonym for Arum italicum, a member of the genus Arum. Arum alpinariae is now considered to be a synonym for Arum italicum, and both names are used to refer to the same species.

intro:
There is no information on "Arum alpinariae." However, I found information on Arum maculatum, also known as Cuckoo Pint or Lords and Ladies. 

Arum maculatum is a flowering perennial plant that belongs to the Arum genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Arum maculatum include alkaloids, such as colchicine, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Arum maculatum is often used to make herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
